channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: President Trump announced Sunday that the U.S. would be creating a crypto reserve to boost the growing industry. “A U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: This mini PC rivals Apple's Mac Studio and Nvidia's DIGITS desktop supercomputer but will cost only a fraction of the price
item.description: Framework Desktop is a customizable mini PC powered by AMD Ryzen AI Max processors and up to 128GB RAM.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Leaked iPhone 17 schematics show the Apple phones could be getting a speaker redesign
item.description: It seems the iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 17 Pro Max could be in line for a modified speaker configuration.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Your iPhone has a hidden folder eating up storage space without you even knowing
item.description: iPhones have a hidden folder that eats up storage without you knowing it. Kurt the CyberGuy offers some tricks to free up storage on your device.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House to host first Crypto summit
item.description: The White House is set to welcome professionals in the cryptocurrency industry on March 7 for a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Samsung Galaxy tri-fold – 5 key rumored features, from its screen size to its hinge
item.description: Samsung has only hinted at the existence of a tri-fold Galaxy phone, but that hasn’t stopped tipsters and leakers from providing us with a wealth of rumors about the supposed new addition to the Samsung lineup.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Intel’s anticipated $28 billion chip factories in Ohio are delayed until 2030
item.description: Intel‘s promised $28 billion chip fabrication plants in Ohio are facing further delays, with the first factory in New Albany expected to not be completed until 2030, local media outlet The Columbus Dispatch reported on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Two AI developer strategies: Hire engineers or let AI do the work
item.description: The stark difference in the way tech giants in China and the US are approaching AI for internal operations was illustrated late this week by separate announcements from Salesforce and Alibaba.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Around $40 billion worth of illicit crypto transactions took place in 2024
item.description: Record figure is likely to rise even higher in 2025 as new information on historical activity emerges.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is this the perfect portable router? GL-iNet's latest model offers dual SIM, load balancing, gigabit LAN ports and four massive antennas
item.description: GL.iNet’s Spitz Plus GL-X2000 merges dual SIM speeds with bandwidth bonding, offering a fast, stable Wi-Fi 6 connection for mobile users and remote locations.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: The people who once ran the federal weather and oceans agency say it touches people’s daily lives in unnoticed ways and that massive firings there could cause needless deaths and a big hit to America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chip Ganassi Racing partners with OpenAI in first motorsports venture for AI company
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Chip Ganassi Racing on Friday said it has partnered on a strategic research collaboration with OpenAI — a deal that makes the two-time reigning IndyCar championship winning team the first to bring in a partner from the artificial intelligence field.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: East Bay startup’s new stove that plugs into a normal wall outlet could be major gain for health and climate
item.description: The sleek, standard four-burner electric induction stove runs on 120 volts, meaning there is no need to pay a licensed electrician thousands of dollars to rewire to 240 volts, which many electric stoves require.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011 and which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Santa Cruz-based Joby Aviation expects first air taxi passengers in late 2025, early 2026
item.description: The company also reported it received more than $1 billion of additional funding and commitments in the fourth quarter.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple unveils 'age assurance' technology amid child safety push
item.description: Apple will introduce "age assurance" technology to boost children's privacy and safety on the company's devices, the company announced Thursday as part of its broader push to protect kids online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Overcoming data compliance and security challenges in the age of AI
item.description: We are in the era of artificial intelligence (AI), and businesses are unlocking unprecedented opportunities for growth and efficiency.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NFL NextGen Stats team unveils new tool to help fans better understand combine results
item.description: The NFL scouting combine provides a blizzard of data with hundreds of prospects running through various drills ranging from the 40-yard dash to the vertical jump to a short shuttle.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: StarlingX bridges IPv4-IPv6 gap with dual-stack networking support
item.description: The Open Infrastructure Foundation is out with the release of StarlingX 10.0, a significant update to the open-source distributed cloud platform designed for IoT, 5G, O-RAN and edge computing applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Just what is an ‘IT worker’ now? The definition is changing
item.description: Not so long ago, the definition of an IT worker was pretty simple: they were largely confined to central roles related to hardware, network infrastructure, and software development in centralized IT departments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas. The fast-growing economy now aims to get 16% of its energy from the sun — more than triple its earlier target of just 5%.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Kandji exec: Vision Pro is a good fit for the enterprise
item.description: Apple’s recently announced partnership with Dassault Systemés shows the Vision Pro as the cutting-edge enterprise solution it has become, but business users will want to ensure these devices can be managed like the rest of their critical IT infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s .NET 10 arrives in first preview
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out the first preview of .NET 10, a major new version of the company’s flagship software development platform. Likely to arrive as a production release in November, .NET 10 introduces C# 14 and adds major enhancements to the .NET runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 11 rules for writing better code
item.description: You can’t develop software and manage software projects for 30 years and not learn a thing or two or twelve about writing good code. Here are a near-dozen nuggets of coding wisdom I picked up along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco, Nvidia expand AI partnership to include Silicon One technology
item.description: Cisco and Nvidia have expanded their collaboration to support enterprise AI implementations by tying together Cisco’s Silicon One technology and Nvidia’s Ethernet networking platform.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly, the ranking member of the Oversight and Accountability Committee, said the CIOs should remain career-reserve SES positions. The post Connolly calls on OPM to reverse CIO classification change first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
